content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File No. M-87-218 
Date 
August 11, 1987 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Kathy Donovan 
Subject: SEl Filing - Chief Deputy Treasurer 
The Treasurer's Office has contacted Alice Hughes 
concerning the SEl filing responsibilities of the Chief Deputy 
Treasurer. 
Due to Jesse Unruh's death, there is a vacancy in the 
office of Treasurer. 
The Governor will fill the vacancy by 
appointment; however, the Chief Deputy Treasurer serves as the 
"acting" Treasurer in the interim. 
(Government Code section 
1775, copy attached.) 
The question is whether the Chief Deputy 
Treasurer (a Form 730 filer) has any additional SEl filing 
duties while serving as "acting" Treasurer. 
Specifically, is 
the chief deputy required to file a Form 721, as would be 
required of the Treasurer? 
I concluded that the chief deputy is not required to file 
the Form 721. 
My conclusion is based on Section 1775, which 
provides that the chief deputy shall discharge the duties of 
Treasurer after the vacancy in the Treasurer's office has 
occurred and prior to the time such vacancy is filled. 
Section 
1775 indicates that the office of Treasurer remains vacant 
until filled by the Governor. 
Accordingly, the chief deputy is 
not considered to hold the position of Treasurer during the 
period that the office is vacant. 
Therefore, the chief deputy 
has no additional SEl filing duties as a result of the vacancy. 
Enclosure 
KED:jaj 
cc: 
Diane Griffiths 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Alice Hughes 


§: 1775. 
SU(JCrintendent of puhlic instruction; lieutenant gov-
ernor; 
se<'re~lry of stat(); controller; treasurer; at-
torney general; st~lt(l board of equalization 
Whenever there is a vacancy in t he office of the SU(JCrintendent 
of Public Instruction, the Lieutenant Governor. Senetary of State, 
Controller, Treasw'er, or Attorney General, or on the State Board of 
Equalization, the Governor shall nominate a person to fill the vacan-
cy who shall take office upon confil'mation by a majority of the mem-
bership of the Senate and a majority of the membership of the As-
sembly and who shall hold office f(ll' the balance of the unexpired 
term, In the event the nominee is neither confirmed nor refused con-
firmation by both the Senate and the Assembly within 90 days of the 
submission of the nomination, the nominee shall take office as if he 
or she had been confirmed by a majority of the Senate and Assem-
bly; provided, that if such 90-day period ends during a recess of the 
Legislature, the period shall be extended until the sixth day following 
the day on which the Legislature reconvenes, 
After a vacancy has occurred in an office specified in this section 
and prior to the time such vacancy is filled as provided in this section, 
the chief deputy to the above constitutional officers shall discharge 
the duties of the office, 
(Stat!-l.194:~. c. l:l·l, p. 971, ~ 1775. Amended by Stats.1977, c. %, p. 517, ~ 
1.)

sections.question: The question is whether the Chief Deputy Treasurer (a Form 730 filer) has any additional SEl filing duties while serving as "acting" Treasurer. Specifically, is the chief deputy required to file a Form 721, as would be required of the Treasurer?
sections.conclusion: I concluded that the chief deputy is not required to file the Form 721. My conclusion is based on Section 1775, which provides that the chief deputy shall discharge the duties of Treasurer after the vacancy in the Treasurer's office has occurred and prior to the time such vacancy is filled. Section 1775 indicates that the office of Treasurer remains vacant until filled by the Governor. Accordingly, the chief deputy is not considered to hold the position of Treasurer during the period that the office is vacant. Therefore, the chief deputy has no additional SEl filing duties as a result of the vacancy.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Chief Deputy Treasurer who assumes acting duties during a vacancy in the Treasurer's office must file Form 721 (SEl disclosure) in addition to their Form 730 filing requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Chief Deputy Treasurer is not required to file Form 721 because under Government Code Section 1775, the office of Treasurer remains vacant until filled by the Governor, and the chief deputy does not hold the position despite discharging its duties.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is internal FPPC memorandum with clear question and conclusion sections. References Government Code Section 1775 regarding succession duties. No formal headers but content clearly delineates Q&C.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The question is whether the Chief Deputy Treasurer (a Form 730 filer) has any additional SEl filing duties while serving as "acting" Treasurer. Specifically, is the chief deputy required to file a Form 721, as would be required of the Treasurer?

CONCLUSION: I concluded that the chief deputy is not required to file the Form 721. My conclusion is based on Section 1775, which provides that the chief deputy shall discharge the duties of Treasurer after the vacancy in the Treasurer's office has occurred and prior to the time such vacancy is filled. Section 1775 indicates that the office of Treasurer remains vacant until filled by the Governor. Accordingly, the chief deputy is not considered to hold the position of Treasurer during the period that the office is vacant. Therefore, the chief deputy has no additional SEl filing duties as a result of the vacancy.

embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum addressing whether a Chief Deputy Treasurer serving in an acting capacity during a Treasurer vacancy has additional SEl filing duties, concluding no Form 721 filing is required because the office legally remains vacant.
